zh.Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Spencer於1546年出生在英國貝德福爾可普爾時，他的父親Thomas Spencer Esq.32歲，母親Anne Buckeley26歲。他於1570年與Frances Mathon在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1589年9月23日在英國素福爾可享年43歲。

zh-hans.Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England: 英国瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Spencer于1546年出生在英国貝德福爾可普爾时，他的父亲Thomas Spencer Esq.32岁，母亲Anne Buckeley26岁。他于1570年与Frances Mathon在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1589年9月23日在英国素福爾可享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Spencer was born in 1546, in Cople, Bedfordshire, England, his father, Thomas Spencer Esq., was 32 and his mother, Anne Buckeley, was 26. He married Frances Mathon in 1570, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 23 September 1589, in Suffolk, England, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Spencer est né en 1546, à Cople, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Spencer Esq., avait 32 ans et sa mère, Anne Buckeley, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Frances Mathon en 1570, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 23 septembre 1589, à Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Spencer 1546 in Cople, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Spencer Esq. 32 und seine Mutter Anne Buckeley 26. Er hat 1570 in Suffolk, England Frances Mathon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 23. September 1589 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Spencer è nato il nel 1546 nel Cople, Bedfordshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Spencer Esq., aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Anne Buckeley, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Frances Mathon nel 1570, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 23 settembre 1589, nel Suffolk, England, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.Cople, Bedfordshire, England: イギリスベドフォードシュアイアコプル
ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencerが1546年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアコプルで生まれた時，父Thomas Spencer Esq.は32歳，母Anne Buckeleyは26歳であった。1570年にイギリススフォルクでFrances Mathonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1589年9月23日にイギリススフォルクで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer(이)가 1546년 영국 베드퍼드실르 코플에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Spencer Esq.(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Buckeley(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1570년 영국 수폴크에서 Frances Mathon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1589년 9월 23일 영국 수폴크에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Spencer nasceu em 1546, em Cople, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Spencer Esq., tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Anne Buckeley, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Frances Mathon em 1570, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de setembro de 1589, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Spencer родился в 1546 г. в местности копл, бедфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Spencer Esq. было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Buckeley, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Frances Mathon в 1570 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 23 сентября 1589 г. в местности суффолк, Англия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Spencer nació en 1546, en Cople, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Spencer Esq., tenía 32 años y su mamá, Anne Buckeley, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Frances Mathon en 1570, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 23 de septiembre de 1589, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 43 años.
